questions.text: When you think about the places you've lived — both in the US and Mexico — what are the specific little things that made a place feel 'right' to you beyond just the basics? Like, was it a certain type of light in the apartment, a neighborhood vibe, the sound of the area, something about the building's architecture, or the way the space was laid out?

When you're watching something on a streaming service — do you tend to binge whole seasons in one or two sittings, or are you more of a 'one or two episodes a night' paced watcher? And do you have any specific rituals around TV/movie time — like always with a specific drink or snack, watching with your partner, or unwinding in a particular spot?

When it comes to your financial life — are you someone who tracks every expense and has a detailed budget, or do you more or less just know roughly where your money goes without tracking it rigorously? And do you have any specific money habits or rules that work for you (like 'never spend more than X on a whim,' automate savings, check accounts weekly, etc.)?

Do you have any interest in creative or artistic pursuits outside of work — things like writing, drawing, making music, woodworking, crafting, or any kind of hands-on making? And if you do dabble in anything creative, is it something you do just for yourself and the process, or do you ever share it or want to create things other people could use/see?

When you think about your digital footprint and how you show up online — beyond personal branding for work — do you have any concerns or preferences around privacy? Like, do you try to keep certain parts of your life off social media, are you careful about what personal info you share publicly, or are you pretty open about most things?
